tldr: Hybrid inference pipeline combining Template-Based Modeling (TBM) with Protenix diffusion model predictions, applying adaptive RNA constraints and diversity transforms to generate 5 coordinate samples per RNA sequence.
summary: This notebook implements a two-phase inference pipeline for the Stanford RNA 3D Folding competition. Phase 1 uses Template-Based Modeling (TBM) to align test sequences against the training/validation pool, adapting coordinates and applying diversity transforms alongside adaptive RNA constraints. Phase 2 falls back to the Protenix diffusion model for targets lacking sufficient TBM matches, handling input configuration, coordinate extraction, and robust C1' atom masking. The pipeline combines both sources to produce 5 samples per target, filling gaps with a de-novo A-form RNA helix fallback. The author provides a configuration table showing how toggling MSA, RNA MSA, templates, and TBM affects leaderboard scores, with a best score of 0.249 achieved using only Protenix without templates.
extensive_summary:
## Overview
The notebook implements a hybrid inference pipeline for RNA 3D structure prediction, combining Template-Based Modeling (TBM) with the Protenix diffusion model. It processes test sequences, generates multiple coordinate samples per target, and outputs a submission CSV.

## Phase 1: Template-Based Modeling (TBM)
The pipeline first aligns each test sequence against the combined training and validation sequence pool using `Bio.Align.PairwiseAligner` (global alignment, match=2, mismatch=-1.5, gap scores=-8/-0.4). It retrieves the top 30 similar sequences, filtering by `MIN_SIMILARITY` and `MIN_PERCENT_IDENTITY` (default 50%). For each match, coordinates are adapted to the query sequence via alignment mapping, with gaps filled by linear interpolation or forward/backward extension. To ensure diversity across the 5 required samples, the notebook applies sequential transforms: raw adaptation, Gaussian noise scaling with similarity, hinge rotation, chain jittering, and spline-based wiggle smoothing. Each transformed structure is refined using `adaptive_rna_constraints`, which enforces:
- Backbone bond length ~5.95 Å
- Soft i–i+2 distance ~10.2 Å
- Laplacian smoothing
- Self-avoidance (repulsion < 3.2 Å for residues > 2 apart)
Strength scales with alignment confidence.

## Phase 2: Protenix Inference
Targets that don't reach 5 TBM predictions are routed to Protenix (`protenix_base_20250630_v1.0.0`). The notebook handles Kaggle path resolution, symlinks read-only assets (`common`, `checkpoint`, `mmcif`) to a writable `/kaggle/working/protenix_root` directory, and builds a JSON input file. It dynamically updates Protenix configs (`sample_diffusion.N_sample`) per target, runs inference, and extracts coordinates. A critical robustness step handles C1' atom masking: it checks `centre_atom_mask`, then falls back to `atom_to_tokatom_idx == 11` or `12` based on which count better matches the sequence length. Coordinates are padded/trimmed to the full (un-truncated) sequence length, with collapse detection (identical consecutive coordinates) resetting to zeros.

## Phase 3: Combination & Fallback
TBM and Protenix predictions are stacked to exactly `N_SAMPLE=5` per target. Remaining slots are filled with a de-novo A-form RNA helix (`generate_rna_structure`) using fixed rise (2.5 Å) and twist (0.6 rad), followed by the same adaptive constraints. Final coordinates are clipped to [-999.999, 9999.999] and saved.

## Configuration & Results
The author tracks leaderboard scores across 7 versions by toggling `USE_MSA`, `USE_RNA_MSA`, `USE_TEMPLATE`, and `TBM`. The best score (0.249 LB) was achieved with version 4 (all flags False, TBM disabled). Version 3 (TBM=True, others False) scored 0.408. The notebook emphasizes path setup, deterministic seeding, and graceful degradation for model failures.
preprocessing: C1' atom mask extraction (centre_atom_mask -> atom_to_tokatom_idx 11/12 fallback)
Coordinate padding/trimming to match sequence length
ensemble: Combines TBM predictions and Protenix diffusion samples to reach 5 samples per target, filling remaining slots with a de-novo A-form RNA helix fallback.
post_processing: Adaptive RNA constraints (bond lengths, i-i+2 distances, Laplacian smoothing, self-avoidance), coordinate clipping, model collapse detection (resetting identical coordinates to zeros), padding/trimming to full sequence length.
what_worked: Version 4 (TBM=False, all MSA/template flags False) achieved the best leaderboard score of 0.249.
what_did_not_work: Version 3 (TBM=True, others False) scored 0.408, indicating TBM alone or with limited MSA was less effective than pure Protenix inference.
critical_findings: TBM alignment quality heavily depends on sequence identity thresholds; model collapse (identical coordinates) can occur and requires explicit detection and zero-resetting; C1' atom indexing varies between featurizers (11 vs 12), requiring a length-matching fallback heuristic.
notable_techniques: Adaptive RNA constraints enforcing ~5.95Å backbone bonds, ~10.2Å i-i+2 distances, Laplacian smoothing, and distance-based self-avoidance
Diversity transforms for TBM predictions (hinge rotation, chain jittering, spline wiggle)
Dynamic Protenix config updates per target sequence length
C1' atom mask fallback using atom_to_tokatom_idx 11/12 count matching
De-novo A-form RNA helix fallback with fixed 2.5Å rise and 0.6 rad twist
